description: Canonical shared-resource coordination platform for local multi-project development. It reuses existing DevGov dashboard, service-status, service-control, report, and operating-system observation surfaces before introducing new coordination mechanisms.

platform.observationSurface: DevGov /api/resource-coordination plus operating-system process and resource summaries
platform.notes: This platform is a common read model for agents and operators. It does not schedule, stop, restart, or throttle work by default.

freshness.notes: Resource coordination status must be time-bounded. Stale snapshots or claims are historical evidence only and must not be used as current congestion or ownership facts.

channels.notes: Primary existing shared status surface for registered services, ports, local agents, and readiness rows.
Generated local evidence for shared host pressure and contention disposition. Reports may include local process-family counts but not command lines, secrets, or credential paths.
Existing server-authored local control log. Use it as evidence of reviewed control actions, not as a scheduler queue.
Existing local dashboard event sink for sanitized observation events. Do not use it for control commands or approval.

channels.surface: Operating-system CPU, memory, and process-family summaries
channels.notes: Use built-in host observation mechanisms first. Canonical records must not store process IDs, full commands, usernames, paths, or secret-bearing environment values.

signals.source: operator observation across concurrent Codex development sessions
signals.interpretation: Lag, timeout, browser delay, or slow tool response is a degraded observation signal, not direct proof that the target project process is unstable.
signals.notes: Agents must compare target-local evidence with shared host pressure before assigning cause.

signals.source: DevGov service-status, health procedures, Doctor results, and project logs
signals.interpretation: Target instability requires target-local evidence such as failing health checks, reproducible errors, crashed listeners, or explicit Doctor failures.
signals.notes: A healthy target under host pressure should be classified as environment-contention or unknown-degraded, not target-unhealthy.

signals.source: Operating-system CPU, memory, disk, GPU, process, and browser profile observations
signals.interpretation: High shared host pressure can explain lag across otherwise healthy projects and should be recorded before remediation.
signals.notes: Use available built-in counters and existing DevGov snapshots before adding a resident monitor.

signals.source: DevGov service-control and dashboard execution status
signals.interpretation: Reviewed control actions can provide future scheduling or queue evidence, but they are not an automatic throttle in v1.
signals.notes: Scheduling requires a separate reviewed apply path and operator-approved policy.

exclusiveResources.resource: Browser automation profile or authenticated browser session
exclusiveResources.registrationSurface: resource-coordination snapshot or sanitized dashboard observation event
exclusiveResources.useGate: Register intended browser/profile use before automation when concurrent agents may share the same browser, cookies, extension state, or DevTools session.
exclusiveResources.notes: Claims must not include cookie values, session data, profile paths, or credentials.

exclusiveResources.resource: GPU-heavy 3D rendering, WebGL, WebGPU, canvas verification, video rendering, or model inference
exclusiveResources.registrationSurface: resource-coordination snapshot or sanitized dashboard observation event
exclusiveResources.useGate: Register expected GPU or rendering-heavy work before starting long visual checks or render loops.
exclusiveResources.notes: Prefer bounded runs, close render surfaces after verification, and record only project/service intent in canonical data.

exclusiveResources.resource: Foreground screen, pointer, keyboard, simulator, or interactive desktop control
exclusiveResources.registrationSurface: resource-coordination snapshot or sanitized dashboard observation event
exclusiveResources.useGate: Register screen or foreground-control use before tasks that can steal focus, type into windows, resize displays, or interfere with another agent's visual inspection.
exclusiveResources.notes: Do not store screenshots, window titles containing private data, or personal activity in canonical registry data.

policies.requirement: Before treating lag as a target project failure, inspect target health evidence and shared host pressure.
policies.enforcement: Classify degraded observations as target-unhealthy, environment-contention, or unknown-degraded; avoid remediation until the classification is evidence-backed.
policies.notes: This prevents unnecessary restarts, edits, or rollback work when the host is simply overloaded by concurrent development.

policies.requirement: Use existing local operating-system monitors, DevGov dashboard state, service-status rows, service-control logs, and report artifacts before adding new resident services.
policies.enforcement: Any new coordinator, queue, or scheduler must explain why the existing DevGov and OS mechanisms are insufficient.
policies.notes: This keeps resource coordination from becoming another heavy always-on dependency.

policies.requirement: Automatic throttling, pausing, restarting, killing, priority changes, or cross-project scheduling requires a separate reviewed apply path.
policies.enforcement: The v1 platform may observe and communicate status only. It must not mutate runtime state or allocate resources automatically.
policies.notes: Future scheduling can build on this registry but must pass service-control and operator approval gates.

policies.requirement: Register intended use before taking browser profiles, GPU-heavy rendering, foreground screen control, or other capacity-limited exclusive resources.
policies.enforcement: Use the resource-coordination surface or an approved report artifact to publish a sanitized claim before starting work; if a conflicting active claim exists, pause or negotiate instead of assuming the target process is unstable.
policies.notes: Registration is a coordination signal, not permission to reveal secrets, capture private screens, or bypass higher approval gates.

policies.requirement: Every resource snapshot and exclusive-resource claim must have a generated, observed, or refreshed timestamp plus an expiry rule.
policies.enforcement: Treat stale status as unknown-degraded or historical evidence; refresh observation before diagnosing current congestion, active ownership, or scheduling priority.
policies.notes: This prevents old resource claims from blocking current work.

stages.name: Observe
stages.trigger: Lag, timeout, slow browser automation, delayed health response, or user-reported contention.
stages.allowedActions: Read existing dashboard state, service-status, generated resource snapshots, and operating-system summaries.
stages.requiredGate: No extra approval for read-only observation inside the approved workspace.
stages.notes: This is the default stage for LLMs participating in concurrent local development.

stages.name: Communicate
stages.trigger: Multiple projects or agents need a shared explanation of current resource pressure.
stages.allowedActions: Write sanitized local report artifacts or dashboard observation events without command lines, secrets, or credential paths.
stages.requiredGate: Use existing report and dashboard event surfaces; do not turn communication into control approval.
stages.notes: This provides a common message board/read model without a new resident broker.

stages.name: Schedule
stages.trigger: Operator requests active coordination, throttling, or priority management across project work.
stages.allowedActions: Design a reviewed scheduler or queue that calls only approved service-control actions.
stages.requiredGate: Separate explicit operator approval, rollback plan, privacy review, and service-control policy review.
stages.notes: Blocked by design until a later scheduling proposal is reviewed.
